metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: Destructive/irreversible operation(s) with no stated safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: State the safeguard (backup, dry-run, reversible migration, rollback) or justify the irreversibility explicitly (T4).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the plan title to reflect all five stale spots, e.g. 'Purge stale bash-engine language from CI comments & _commands module docstrings'.
findings.finding: The plan title names only 'CI comments & leaf.py docstring' but the body (and AC) cover two additional files discovered mid-planning — _seam.py and __init__.py — making the title materially undersell the actual scope.
findings.location: Plan title vs. Scope section / spots 4–5
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'Purge stale bash-engine language from CI comments & _commands module docstrings (leaf.py, _seam.py, __init__.py)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the `__init__.py` edit to also reword the `main()` function docstring (line 67, 'bash dispatcher's Python leaf-write route') to describe its current role as the CLI entry for the in-process command dispatch.
findings.finding: The `__init__.py` `main()` function docstring (line 67: 'CLI entry for the bash dispatcher's Python leaf-write route') and the hard-coded usage string (line 74: 'Usage: ticket-commands.py <command> [args...]') are stale bash-era text inside the target file, but the plan's stated scope for `__init__.py` is only lines 1–10 (the module docstring). The `main()` function docstring at line 67 will remain stale after the planned edit.
findings.location: src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py — `main()` docstring line 67 and usage string line 74; plan 'Scope' section
findings.suggested_fix: Change the `main()` docstring to something like: 'CLI entry for the in-process command dispatcher. `argv` is `[<command>, <args>...]`. Returns the process exit code; a `CommandError` prints its message to stderr and yields its return code.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Audit the `langfuse-trace` job's `jq` install step (external-integration.yml line 93–94) and either add a correct justification or remove it, mirroring the decision made for the `external` job's jq step.
findings.finding: The `jq` install step exists in BOTH jobs of `external-integration.yml` — the `external` job (line 47–48, which the plan addresses) AND the `langfuse-trace` job (line 93–94, which the plan does not mention). The stale 'bash engine' justification comment is only on the `external` job, but the `langfuse-trace` job's jq install is equally unjustified: a search of `tests/external/` for `jq` returns zero hits, and the `rebar_repo` fixture is pure Python. The plan enumerates only one of the two jq install steps.
findings.location: .github/workflows/external-integration.yml line 93–94 (langfuse-trace job); plan 'Verified stale spots' item 2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the completeness-proof grep in AC #4 to use `grep -Ern` (or `egrep -rn`) so `|` is treated as alternation, not a literal pipe character.
findings.finding: The completeness-proof grep command in AC #4 uses bare `|` without `-E` (or `-P`), so POSIX/GNU grep treats it as a literal pipe character rather than alternation. The command will silently match nothing regardless of whether the stale strings remain, making the 'returns NOTHING' criterion a false pass.
findings.suggested_fix: grep -Ern 'REBAR_LEAF_WRITES|ticket-append-event\.sh|per-command bash suite|route through the bash core|test_bash_suites|bash engine' src/rebar/_commands .github/workflows/test.yml .github/workflows/external-integration.yml

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align AC #2 with the Scope section by stating the default explicitly: 'reword the jq step's justification to describe current usage; do not remove the jq install step (removal is out of scope).'
findings.finding: AC #2 contains a conditional ('jq kept only if still used') that leaves the executor to investigate jq usage before the criterion can be satisfied, without stating what to do if the investigation cannot be completed in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #2 / Scope section (external-integration.yml jq step)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(jq kept only if still used)' in AC #2 with '(the jq install step is kept; only its comment justification is reworded)' to match the Scope's stated default.

findings.finding: AC2's verification grep (`grep -rn "test_bash_suites" .github src`) only validates removal of the test.yml stale spot; it would pass even if the other two stale spots (external-integration.yml line 45-46 and leaf.py line 9) were left untouched, because those use different text (`bash engine`, `per-command bash suite`). The AC is self-inconsistent as a completeness check.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add two more grep checks:
- `grep -n 'bash engine' .github/workflows/external-integration.yml` → no output
- `grep -n 'per-command bash suite' src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py` → no output

findings.finding: The plan scopes the leaf.py fix to 'line ~9' only, but the same docstring block (lines 1-9) contains additional present-tense bash-machinery claims that are equally stale: line 4 references 'reached behind REBAR_LEAF_WRITES=python' (retired per bash-migration.md §kill-switches table), lines 5-7 reference the bash write seam ('_seam.append_event → ticket-append-event.sh → write_commit_event') whose bash leg was deleted in chilly-hyena-jet (bash-migration.md:272). Fixing only line 9 leaves the mechanism incomplete — the docstring will still assert present-tense bash seam machinery after the edit.
findings.location: src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py lines 1-9 (plan section: 'Files' and 'Genuinely-stale spots to fix' bullet 3)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the leaf.py module docstring to remove all present-tense bash-seam language. For example, update lines 4-9 from describing 'reached behind REBAR_LEAF_WRITES=python … appends through the bash write seam … so the per-command bash suite passes against either implementation' to something like: 'They validate and compose in Python, then append through the Python write core (`_seam.append_event` → `event_append.append_event`). The bash seam and kill-switch were retired in the Tier D cutover (docs/bash-migration.md §chilly-hyena-jet).'

findings.finding: AC2's grep command (`grep -rn "test_bash_suites" .github src`) proves only one of the three stale spots (the test.yml reference). The stale bash-engine language in external-integration.yml:45-46 and the leaf.py:9 docstring have no concrete proving command, leaving their removal unverified by the stated ACs.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to Acceptance Criteria:
- `grep -n 'bash engine' .github/workflows/external-integration.yml` returns no output (or only a comment-free line).
- `grep -n 'byte-for-byte\|per-command bash suite' src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py` returns no output.

findings.finding: AC #2's grep (`grep -rn "test_bash_suites" .github src`) verifies removal of only one of the three named stale phrases; the other two stale phrases — "bash engine, which shells out to jq + git" (external-integration.yml) and "per-command bash suite passes against either implementation" (leaf.py) — have no machine-verifiable acceptance criterion, leaving two of the three fixes unverifiable by the stated grep.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC #2 (or as separate ACs): `grep -n 'bash engine' .github/workflows/external-integration.yml` returns no result; `grep -n 'bash suite passes' src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py` returns no result.

findings.finding: Two of the three stale spots (external-integration.yml line 45-46 and leaf.py line 9) lack a mechanically verifiable end-state proof; only the test_bash_suites change has a grep-based AC, leaving the other two reliant on subjective 'no longer assert' inspection.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add two more AC lines:
- `grep -n 'bash engine' .github/workflows/external-integration.yml` returns no present-tense assertion about store initialization via the bash engine.
- `grep -n 'byte-for-byte\|bash suite' src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py` returns no match.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
